top_opportunities.title: Use JuliaLowering.jl for a cleaner way to transform resumable functions to finite state machines [$600]
top_opportunities.body_preview: Edit: #100 has fixed most of these issues without relying on the not-yet-published JuliaLowering.jl. It uses a local reimplementation of most scoping rules of the language, thus missing edge cases might exist. Leaving this (now smaller) bounty for future work of moving this implementation to JuliaLowering, to provide a more centralized source-of-truth on scoping. --- This software transform annotated julia functions into finite state machines that can be paused and re-entered. The issue tracker

top_opportunities.title: Make it easier to write reliable cleanup tasks
top_opportunities.body_preview: [](https://issuehunt.io/r/avajs/ava/issues/928) See: https://github.com/avajs/ava/pull/918#issuecomment-227016238 `after.always` has some issues when used as a cleanup task. Specifically, it won't run if: - There are test failures and `--fail-fast` is used. - There are uncaught exceptions thrown. I've advocated using `.before` or `.beforeEach` to ensure s

top_opportunities.body_preview: [](https://issuehunt.io/r/FAForever/server/issues/640) The server should not be sending any strings that get rendered in the client, because these strings are in English and we can't be sure that the user on the other end has their client set to English (in fact a majority of people probably knows English only as a second language, or does no

top_opportunities.title: [Feature] Limit Order Execution via Off-Chain Keepers
top_opportunities.body_preview: **Description:** Currently, TradeFlow only supports market orders. We want to allow users to place limit orders. The core contract must store the order intent, allowing off-chain "keepers" to execute the trade when the market price meets the order criteria. **Context / Motivation:** Advanced traders require limit orders. Moving this on-chain allows TradeFlow to capture a more sophisticated demographic of DeFi users while rewarding keepers for network maintenance. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] I

top_opportunities.title: Add `GitHubIssuePreviewCard` live data fetch from GitHub API
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Problem `GitHubIssuePreviewCard` renders a GitHub issue preview but may be using static data or mocked content instead of fetching live from the GitHub REST API. ## Scope Wire the card to fetch issue metadata (title, labels, state, open date) from `https://api.github.com/repos/:owner/:repo/issues/:number` on mount. ## Acceptance criteria - Card fetches live issue data on mount using the `repo` and `issueNumber` from the bounty - Loading skeleton shown during fetch - Error state shown with lin

top_opportunities.title: [Bounty: 300 beenz] [Feature] [Crypto] Kubernetes for Dogs (on the blockchain)
top_opportunities.body_preview: For too long our canine companions have been unjustly deprived of the opportunity to agilely nete kubers. Without access to hyperfungible IoT fog-computing, they will forever be left behind in the stone age. Imagine being able to deploy Fido his very own cloud-native AJAX single-page REST API, all from the touchscreen of your Juicero. The industry must be disrupted with blockchain-enabled ultrascale agentic quantum hypervisors for deploying serverless femptoservices to the metaverse. For dogs.

top_opportunities.title: L2: Implement Solana chain module
top_opportunities.body_preview: ## Level: L2 (1-2 days) | Chain: Rust Systems & L2 ### What Arka lists Solana as a "Planned" chain. Implement the Solana backend. ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New module `chain/solana.rs` implementing the Chain trait - [ ] Support: connection, balance check, SOL transfer, SPL token transfer - [ ] Uses `solana-client` crate - [ ] Tests with `solana-test-validator` - [ ] Update `examples/multi_chain.rs` - [ ] README updated ### Rewards Contributions are recognized through the [kcolbchain contribu

top_opportunities.title: [13.12 BTC BOUNTY] implement zero-latency continuous time banana puddingital signal processing library
top_opportunities.body_preview: this feature is part of the banana pudding epic but should not be blocked by anything important. this feature has specific requirements: - we need to use phase-aligned bananas in each pudding batch to minimize subtractive banana flavor interference - nilla wafer cepstral coefficients should always correlate with banana ripeness unless the bananas have been frozen, in which case we can assume a quefrency of 1 - if zero-latency feature extraction is not possible, use multiples of a banana bunch fo
